chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No. D-329 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case. 1. For orders on office objections at Flag ‘A’. 2. For orders on CMA 1446/2023 (stay) 3. For hearing of main case. 17.10.2023. Mr. Muhammad Salim Tunio, Advocate for the petitioner. -.-.-.- The Petitioner is a contractor and professes to be aggrieved as the funds said to be necessary for completion of the work awarded by the Respondents Nos. 4 to 6 in pursuance of certain tenders have allegedly not been released, with it being pleaded that certain works have already been performed for which the Petitioner has yet to be compensated. The Petition has been filed in that backdrop, seeking the allocation/release of such funds. Having considered the matter, we are of the view that Petitioner is essentially seeking the performance of contractual obligations, which does not present a fit case for issuance of a writ under Article 199 of the Constitution and would at best form the subject of a file civil suit. That being so, the Petition stands dismissed, leaving the petitioner at liberty to avail such civil remedy, if so desired. JUDGE JUDGE Irfan/PA
